title: shlex injection guard in bot scripting is correct and well-tested
description: scripting.rs uses shlex::split + Command::new (not sh -c) to prevent shell injection, drains stdout/stderr concurrently to prevent deadlock on pipe-full, enforces a hard timeout, and caps output at 64KiB/8KiB. All four properties are independently tested. A contributor might suggest simplifying to sh -c for convenience; the existing design correctly prevents command injection when the hook string comes from user config.
